key_findings: Zn and its alloys are promising biodegradable metallic materials for clinical applications due to suitable degradation rate, bioresorbable degradation products, and excellent biocompatibility.
The strength and ductility of Zn alloys are generally below the mechanical benchmark for biodegradable implants (e.g., bone fixtures and stents).
Alloying elements such as Mg, Ca, Sr, Mn, Li, Ag, Cu, Fe, and rare earth elements (Er, Dy, Ho) are used to improve mechanical properties of Zn.
Zn-2Ca alloy showed superior comprehensive performance with hardness 90.94 HV and corrosion rate ~0.074-0.084 mm/year.
As-rolled Zn-1Mg alloy with 0.1 wt% Mn addition achieved UTS of 300 MPa and elongation of 25%.
Multi-component Zn alloys (e.g., Zn-Cu-Ag, Zn-Mg-Sr, Zn-Li-Mg) are being developed to optimize mechanical, antibacterial, and degradation properties.
optimization_goals: Improve mechanical strength and ductility of Zn alloys for biodegradable implant applications.
Achieve suitable degradation rate and biocompatibility for clinical use.
Optimize composition and microstructure for reproducibility and biocompatibility.
notes: The provided paper content consists of fragmented sections from multiple research papers, lacking a single coherent study. No specific alloy composition, processing parameters, or quantitative mechanical/corrosion data for a single alloy could be extracted. The key findings are synthesized from general statements across sections.

key_findings: Zn and its alloys are promising biodegradable metallic materials due to suitable degradation rate, biocompatibility, and biofunctionality.
Pure Zn has low mechanical strength (UTS ≤30 MPa, elongation ≤0.25-3%), insufficient for implant applications.
Alloying elements such as Mg, Ca, Sr, Mn, Ge, Ag, Cu, and rare earth elements (Er, Dy, Ho) are used to improve mechanical properties.
Zn-Mg-RE alloys are specifically mentioned as a system of interest.
Mechanical properties of Zn alloys can be enhanced through thermomechanical processing (e.g., hot extrusion, rolling).
For biodegradable stents, a UTS >300 MPa is a benchmark; for general implant materials, UTS >200 MPa and elongation >15% are required.
As-cast Zn alloys generally have mechanical properties far below these benchmarks.
Addition of 0.1 wt% Mn to as-rolled Zn-1Mg alloy improved UTS from 210 to 300 MPa and elongation from 8% to 25%.
optimization_goals: Improve strength and ductility of Zn alloys to meet mechanical benchmarks for biodegradable implants (bone fixtures, stents).
Achieve suitable corrosion resistance and biocompatibility alongside mechanical performance.
notes: The provided text consists of fragmented sections from multiple papers, not a single coherent study. paper_title: mechanical properties in Al-Zn-Mg-Cu aluminum alloy: strain-induced back stress strengthening
key_findings: 基于有限证据推测：论文主题为Al-Zn-Mg-Cu铝合金中应变诱导背应力强化
notes: 提供的证据仅为论文标题、作者、期刊、DOI和年份，未包含具体的成分、工艺、性能等详细数据。

microstructure.precipitates: coarse and discontinuous grain boundary precipitates

key_findings: Zn/Mg stoichiometry controls the balance between strength and corrosion resistance in dilute Al-Zn-Mg alloys.
An alloy with Zn/Mg ratio of 3.68 exhibited a corrosion sensitivity index of 3.6% and grain boundary precipitate size of 15.3 µm, attributed to coarse and discontinuous grain boundary precipitates.
Decreasing Zn/Mg ratio significantly increases strength, SCC resistance, and electrochemical corrosion resistance in Al-Zn-Mg alloys with Zn+Mg ≈ 7 wt%.

Addition of 0.04 wt% Ce to Al-Zn-Mg alloy significantly reduces self-corrosive current density and increases passivation film resistance, lowering stress corrosion sensitivity index from 0.612 to 0.219.
optimization_goals: Balance between strength and corrosion resistance
Improve corrosion resistance while maintaining mechanical properties
notes: The paper content is fragmented across multiple sections and includes references to multiple alloy systems (Al-Zn-Mg, Mg-Sc-Zn, Mg-Zn-La). The extracted data focuses on Al-Zn-Mg alloys where specific Zn/Mg ratios and processing details are mentioned. The melting temperature of 760°C is from Section 1 for Al-Zn-Mg alloys. The Zn/Mg ratio of 3.68 and corrosion sensitivity index of 3.6% are from Section 6. The effect of Ce addition is from Section 7. The effect of Zn/Mg ratio on strength and corrosion is from Section 8. No specific composition percentages (e.g., exact Zn, Mg, Al wt%) are provided for a single alloy, only ratios and trends.

Zn-based alloys with additions of Ti and Cu exhibit good creep resistance and improved mechanical properties compared with pure Zn
Ti is considered as effective alloying element to further optimize the degradation rate and mechanical properties of Zn-Cu alloys
The effect of Ti on biodegradation behavior of Zn-Cu alloys has not been investigated prior to this work
optimization_goals: Optimize degradation rate and mechanical properties of Zn-Cu alloys via Ti addition
